nodes.cmd: uip or jobs list --folder-key {{shared_folder_key}} --state Faulted --output json

nodes._doc: RemoteException carries NO DAP code — it is an out-of-process wrapper. Only treat as a connector failure when the faulted activity is a connector activity (Connector*/UiPath.IntegrationService.Activities.*). Both predicates are exact (FQN/namespace token match).
Unwrap to the innermost message: strip the 'RemoteException:' prefix and follow every '--->' to the deepest inner exception text. Classify on inner_message, never on the RemoteException class name.

Inner-message classification is judgment: the message is free-form provider/runtime text, not a structured token. The LLM picks the branch whose semantic class the unwrapped text best supports, citing the verbatim inner message.

children.judgment_q: Does the innermost message indicate the connection's OAuth/access token could not be obtained or refreshed (e.g. 'Could not obtain access token', 'invalid_grant', 'Client ID or Client Secret is incorrect', scope/membership errors) — i.e. an authentication failure rather than transport or a downstream HTTP status?

Does the innermost message indicate a transport-layer failure (connection forcibly closed, cannot read from transport, SSL not established, operation/HttpClient timeout) rather than an auth failure or a returned HTTP status code?

when.value: status code does not indicate success: (404|502|503)

children.judgment_q: Does the innermost message report a downstream HTTP status (404 Not Found, 502 Bad Gateway, 503 Service Unavailable) returned by the external service? If 404, the referenced resource does not exist; if 502/503, treat as transient external/platform availability.

nodes.cause: Connector activity faulted out-of-process; unwrapped inner message is a token/auth failure ('{{inner_message}}') — the connection's OAuth token could not be obtained or refreshed (revoked grant, wrong client credentials, bad scopes, or owning identity removed).

Connector activity faulted out-of-process; unwrapped inner message is a transport/SSL/timeout failure ('{{inner_message}}') — usually transient external-service or network/proxy availability, not a workflow defect.

nodes._doc: Downstream HTTP: 404 means missing resource (verify the identifier); 5xx means transient availability. Judgment because it reads the same free-form inner message for which status it carries.

children.judgment_q: Does the innermost message report HTTP 404 (Not Found) — the referenced record/endpoint does not exist in the external service?
Does the innermost message report HTTP 502/503 — a transient downstream gateway/availability error?

nodes.cause: Connector operation reached the external service which returned 404 (Not Found) — the referenced resource (record/endpoint) does not exist; the ID/path the operation used is wrong.
Connector operation reached the external service which returned 502/503 — transient downstream gateway/availability error; retry, and check the external service status if persistent.

nodes.reason: Faulted activity is a connector activity wrapped in RemoteException, but the unwrapped inner message does not match the playbook's token/auth, transport, or 404/502/503 classes. Read the full verbatim inner message and reason from it before concluding.

RemoteException present but the faulted activity is NOT a connector activity (no Connector*/UiPath.IntegrationService.Activities.* frame). RemoteException is a generic robot↔executor wrapper (UI Automation, CV, process-not-found, assembly-load, missing-file). Route to the matching Orchestrator / runtime playbook — not this one.
Faulted job exception is not UiPath.Ipc/CoreIpc.RemoteException. Route to a different playbook or hand back to triage.
